scored_hypotheses.claim: The text uses a simple substitution cipher on medieval Latin, which would produce glyph entropy ~4.0

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: Glyph entropy (3.86 bits) matches prediction (~4.0), but hapax ratio (70%) is anomalously high for medieval Latin substitution cipher (expected 40-50%), and Zipf exponent (0.89) is too flat compared to natural language (1.0-1.5), indicating the text lacks characteristic linguistic structure of enciphered Latin.
